chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly states, "All implementations for on-chain and off-chain components are available on GitHub at https://github.com/lsd-ucsc/Decentagram." This constitutes providing working links to a public repository.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation uses Ganache (a local Ethereum blockchain testing environment) for gas cost analysis, and the Goerli testnet for latency comparison. While it discusses deployment on L2 chains (Polygon, BNB), the core performance evaluations are conducted in controlled simulation/test environments rather than large-scale physical hardware deployments.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: The paper has a dedicated section (Section III) titled 'System and Threat Model' which formally defines assumptions regarding blockchain consensus failure thresholds ($\le 1/3$ of staked ETH) and TEE operational constraints, providing a clear model.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous, data-driven analysis by quantifying gas costs for registration, subscribing, and publishing (Table II), and analyzing the linear increase in publishing cost based on the number of subscribers. It also compares end-to-end latency against an M&R baseline (Table III).

major_weakness: The experimental rigor is limited to controlled local/testnet simulations rather than large-scale physical testbeds or massive real-world deployments.
